User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application, either a student or a teacher, with their basic profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication (password not stored).

role.description: The role of the user within the application (student or teacher).

languagePreference.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface and content (e.g., 'en', 'es').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

LearningModule.title: LearningModule
LearningModule.description: Represents a structured learning unit or course module within a specific subject.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningModule entity.

title.description: The title of the learning module.

description.description: A brief description of what the module covers.

subject.description: The subject category this module belongs to (e.g., 'Mathematics', 'Science').

order.description: The sequential order of this module within its subject or a learning path.

languageCode.description: The language code for this module's content (e.g., 'en', 'es').

LearningContent.title: LearningContent
LearningContent.description: Represents an individual piece of learning material within a module, such as a lesson, explanation, or visual aid.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningContent entity.

moduleId.description: Reference to the LearningModule this content belongs to. (Relationship: LearningModule 1:N LearningContent)

title.description: The title or heading of the content piece.

contentBody.description: The main textual body of the content, if applicable (e.g., a markdown lesson).

mediaUrl.description: URL to external media if contentType is 'image', 'video', or 'audio'.

orderInModule.description: The sequential order of this content within its parent module.

languageCode.description: The language code for this specific content item (e.g., 'en', 'es').

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents an assessment question presented to students to test their understanding.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

learningContentId.description: Reference to the LearningContent this question is associated with. (Relationship: LearningContent 1:N Question)

questionText.description: The text of the question posed to the student.

options.description: An array of possible answer options for 'multipleChoice' questions.

correctAnswer.description: The correct answer to the question. For multiple choice, this could be the text of the correct option or an identifier.

languageCode.description: The language code for this question (e.g., 'en', 'es').

difficulty.description: The perceived difficulty level of the question (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'hard').

StudentResponse.title: StudentResponse
StudentResponse.description: Records a student's answer to a specific question, including their response and when it was submitted.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentResponse entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the User (student) who submitted this response. (Relationship: User 1:N StudentResponse)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question that was answered. (Relationship: Question 1:N StudentResponse)

responseValue.description: The actual answer provided by the student.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the student submitted their response.

AIAnalysis.title: AIAnalysis
AIAnalysis.description: Stores the results of the AI's analysis of a student's response, including identified issues and generated explanations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIAnalysis entity.

studentResponseId.description: Reference to the StudentResponse that was analyzed. (Relationship: StudentResponse 1:1 AIAnalysis)

analysisResultType.description: The type of understanding identified by the AI (e.g., 'confusion', 'guessing', 'partialKnowledge', 'correct').

explanationText.description: The generated textual explanation provided by the AI to address the student's understanding.

explanationType.description: The format or style of the explanation (e.g., 'simpleLanguage', 'story', 'imageDescription').

imageUrl.description: URL to a supplementary image, if the explanation type involves visual aids.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the AI analysis and explanation were generated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, accessible by the user themselves (read/write) and teachers (read). The `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes `role` for general identification but relies on `/teachers/{teacherId}` for explicit DBAC.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A top-level collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The mere existence of a document here (where `teacherId` matches `request.auth.uid`) grants teacher privileges. No specific data beyond the UID is required, though a full user profile can be mirrored.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique ID of a teacher, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Globally accessible learning modules. All signed-in students and teachers can read. Only teachers can create, update, or delete modules. This structure supports QAPs for listing all available modules.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique ID of the learning module.

definition.description: Individual pieces of learning content nested within specific learning modules. All signed-in students and teachers can read. Only teachers can manage (create, update, delete) content. Includes denormalized `moduleId` for direct content-to-module association, which also supports efficient queries.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent learning module.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique ID of the learning content item.

definition.description: Assessment questions nested within specific learning content. All signed-in students and teachers can read. Only teachers can manage (create, update, delete) questions. Includes denormalized `learningContentId` for direct question-to-content association, supporting efficient queries and QAPs for listing questions within content.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent learning module.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent learning content item.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique ID of the question.

definition.description: Records individual student answers to questions. Students can read/write their own responses. Teachers can read all responses (for dashboard/insights). Crucially, includes `studentId` (already in schema) for authorization independence, allowing `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentId` rules for robust QAPs and atomic operations.

params.name: responseId
params.description: The unique ID of the student's response.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated analyses and explanations tied to student responses. Students can read their own analyses. Teachers can read all analyses. **CRITICAL: This document must include a denormalized `studentId` (copied from the associated `StudentResponse`) for authorization independence, enabling `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentId` in security rules without fetching parent documents.** Also includes `studentResponseId` for 1:1 association.

params.name: analysisId
params.description: The unique ID of the AI analysis.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to adhere strictly to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES, prioritizing Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. 

1.  **Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
    *   **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored under `/users/{userId}`, where `userId` is directly derived from `request.auth.uid`. This path-based ownership inherently provides authorization independence, as users can only access their own document directly. 
    *   **Teacher Roles (`/teachers/{teacherId}`):** A dedicated top-level collection `/teachers/{teacherId}` is used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The mere existence of a document at `/teachers/{request.auth.uid}` explicitly grants teacher privileges, eliminating the need for `get()` calls on `User` profiles to check roles in rules, thus ensuring authorization independence for role checks.
    *   **Student Responses (`/studentResponses/{responseId}`):** To ensure authorization independence, each `StudentResponse` document includes the `studentId` field. This allows security rules to evaluate access (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentId`) without needing to fetch any parent document, supporting atomic operations.
    *   **AI Analysis (`/aiAnalyses/{analysisId}`):** Similarly, each `AIAnalysis` document **critically denormalizes the `studentId`** (copied from its associated `StudentResponse`). This enables direct authorization checks (`request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentId`) within the rules, completely independent of fetching the parent `StudentResponse` or `User` document. 
    *   **Learning Content (`/learningModules/.../questions`):** Learning content, modules, and questions are public for students (read-only) and managed by teachers (read/write). Their hierarchical structure (`/learningModules/{moduleId}/contents/{contentId}/questions/{questionId}`) primarily serves organization and efficient querying, rather than defining unique authorization per parent. Since their access posture is uniform, no specific denormalization for sub-collections is required beyond the inherent rule for the collection itself.

2.  **QAPs (Secure List Operations):**
    *   **Public Content (`/learningModules`, `/learningModules/{moduleId}/contents`, `/learningModules/{moduleId}/contents/{contentId}/questions`):** These collections are designed for broad `list` access by all authenticated users (students and teachers), fulfilling QAPs. Teachers have `create`/`update`/`delete` access, which is controlled by the `isTeacher()` DBAC check.
    *   **Student-Specific Data (`/studentResponses`, `/aiAnalyses`):** For students, `list` operations are secured by explicitly filtering on the denormalized `studentId` (e.g., `db.collection('studentResponses').where('studentId', '==', auth.uid)`). Rules ensure `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentId` for individual document access. For teachers, `list` access is granted across all documents in these collections, secured by the `isTeacher()` DBAC check.
    *   **User Profiles (`/users`):** Students can `list` their own profile, teachers can `list` all profiles, which is handled by a combination of `request.auth.uid == userId` and `isTeacher()` rules, supporting QAPs.

This structure ensures a clear, debuggable, and scalable security model by minimizing rule complexity and maximizing authorization independence through strategic denormalization and structural segregation.
